tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58597,"title":"[ContainerInstance] Docs: Add examples for ContainerGroupProfile, ContainerGroupProfileRevision, and NGroup resource types","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` **Service directory:** `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57490) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57490 added the initial `1.0.0-beta.1` release of the `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` library. It includes four generated provisioning resource types: - `ContainerGroup` — basic container group (has an example in README) - `ContainerGroupProfile` — reusable container group profile - `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` — versioned revision of a container group profile - `NGroup` — N-Group resource for confidential/scale container scenarios ## Gaps Found - No README example for `ContainerGroupProfile` — this resource type enables reusable container templates and is a first-class provisioning resource with no documentation - No README example for `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` — revision management of container group profiles is undocumented - No README example for `NGroup` — an entirely new resource concept ("N-Group") with no explanation or sample usage; the name alone offers no guidance to users - Only one test snippet exists (`Snippet:ContainerInstanceBasic`) covering just `ContainerGroup`; no test-backed samples are provided for the three remaining resource types - The "Authenticate the Client" section in README is empty (a heading with no content) - The "Next steps" section in README is empty 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Understand the existing structure The test backing the current snippet is at: `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/tests/BasicContainerInstanceTests.cs` It uses the `Trycep` provisioning test helper with `#region Snippet:ContainerInstanceBasic` / `#endregion` to supply the snippet shown in the README. Follow the same pattern for the new snippets. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### 2a. Add a `ContainerGroupProfile` + `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` example Add a new test method to `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/tests/BasicContainerInstanceTests.cs` (or a new `BasicContainerInstanceProfileTests.cs` in the same folder): ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateContainerGroupProfileTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceProfile Infrastructure infra = new(); ContainerGroupProfile profile = new(nameof(profile), ContainerGroupProfile.ResourceVersions.V2025_09_01) { ContainerGroupOSType = ContainerInstanceOperatingSystemType.Linux, RestartPolicy = ContainerGroupRestartPolicy.Always, Containers = { new ContainerInstanceContainer { Name = "hello", Image = "mcr.microsoft.com/azuredocs/aci-helloworld", Resources = new ContainerResourceRequirements { Requests = new ContainerResourceRequestsContent { Cpu = 1, MemoryInGB = 1.5 } } } } }; infra.Add(profile); #endregion return infra; }); } [Test] public async Task CreateContainerGroupProfile() { await using Trycep test = CreateContainerGroupProfileTest(); // add expected bicep comparison string here after verifying output test.Compare("""..."""); } ``` > **Note:** Inspect the generated `ContainerGroupProfile.cs` and `ContainerGroupProfileRevision.cs` under `src/Generated/` to determine the correct properties and `ResourceVersions` constants to use, and fill in the expected Bicep output in `test.Compare(...)`. #### 2b. Add an `NGroup` example Add a method showing how to create an `NGroup` resource. Inspect `src/Generated/NGroup.cs` for required properties. Wrap the infrastructure-definition code with: ``` #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceNGroup ... #endregion ``` #### 2c. Add snippet placeholders in the README In `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/README.md`, under the `## Examples` section, add two new subsections **after** the existing "Create a Linux Container Group with a Public IP" subsection: ```markdown ### Create a Container Group Profile TODO: brief description ``` ``` ### Create an NGroup TODO: brief description ``` ``` ``` #### 2d. Fill the empty "Authenticate the Client" section For provisioning libraries, authentication is handled by `azd` at deploy time, not by the library at runtime. Add the following content under the `### Authenticate the Client` heading: ```markdown This library generates Bicep infrastructure definitions and does not connect to Azure at authoring time. Authentication is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) when you run `azd up` or `azd provision` to deploy the generated infrastructure to Azure. ``` #### 2e. Fill the "Next steps" section ```markdown - Explore the [Azure Container Instances documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Learn about [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deploying provisioning infrastructure - Browse the [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning) core library for shared infrastructure primitives ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/README.md` currently contains all required sections, but the following are empty or incomplete: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ | None | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ✅ | None | | Getting started > Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 2d | | Key concepts | ✅ Minimal (consistent with other provisioning libs) | None | | Examples | ⚠️ Only ContainerGroup | Add snippets per Step 2a–2c | | Troubleshooting | ✅ | None | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 2e | | Contributing | ✅ | None | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 containerinstance` — injects `#region Snippet:` code into README placeholder blocks; verify the README diff looks correct 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 containerinstance` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24833660014/agentic_workflow) · ● 747K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58597","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T12:00:27Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T12:00:27Z"}

Fill the empty "Authenticate the Client" section For provisioning libraries, authentication is handled by `azd` at deploy time, not by the library at runtime. Add the following content under the `### Authenticate the Client` heading: ```markdown This library generates Bicep infrastructure definitions and does not connect to Azure at authoring time. Authentication is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) when you run `azd up` or `azd provision` to deploy the generated infrastructure to Azure. ``` #### 2e. Fill the "Next steps" section ```markdown - Explore the [Azure Container Instances documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Learn about [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deploying provisioning infrastructure - Browse the [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning) core library for shared infrastructure primitives ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/README.md` currently contains all required sections, but the following are empty or incomplete: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ | None | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ✅ | None | | Getting started > Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 2d | | Key concepts | ✅ Minimal (consistent with other provisioning libs) | None | | Examples | ⚠️ Only ContainerGroup | Add snippets per Step 2a–2c | | Troubleshooting | ✅ | None | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 2e | | Contributing | ✅ | None | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 containerinstance` — injects `#region Snippet:` code into README placeholder blocks; verify the README diff looks correct 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 containerinstance` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents documentation gaps in the newly released `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` 1.0.0-beta.1 package, specifically missing README examples and test snippets for `ContainerGroupProfile`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevision`, and `NGroup` resource types.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` (1.0.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** `ContainerGroupProfile`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevision`, `NGroup` resource types
**Scenarios:**
- No README example for `ContainerGroupProfile` (reusable container templates)
- No README example for `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` (revision management)
- No README example for `NGroup` (confidential/scale container scenarios)
- Empty "Authenticate the Client" and "Next steps" README sections
- Only `Snippet:ContainerInstanceBasic` exists; no test-backed samples for the three remaining resource types

**Root ask:** Add code snippets (test-backed via `Trycep` pattern) and README content for the three undocumented resource types, and fill in the empty README sections.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue, not a runtime bug. The issue includes a detailed implementation guide.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/tests/BasicContainerInstanceTests.cs` to understand the existing `Trycep` snippet pattern
2. Inspect `src/Generated/ContainerGroupProfile.cs`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevision.cs`, and `NGroup.cs` for available properties and `ResourceVersions` constants
3. Add new test methods with `#region Snippet:ContainerInstanceProfile` / `#region Snippet:ContainerInstanceNGroup` regions
4. Add corresponding `### Create a Container Group Profile` and `### Create an NGroup` subsections to README.md
5. Fill in the empty "Authenticate the Client" and "Next steps" sections
6. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 containerinstance` to inject snippet content into the README
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — The package is `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance`, clearly an Azure.Provisioning.* library
- **Service:** `Container Instances` — The service directory is `sdk/containerinstance/` and the service is Azure Container Instances
- **Confidence:** High — Package name unambiguously identifies both category and service; both labels exist in the repository
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Container Instances` (line 622) — matched because the issue targets the Container Instances service
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @drishtipeshwani09
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners listed)
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan from line 1249 upward; line 622 (`%Container Instances`) was the first match. Line 399 (`%Provisioning` with AzureSdkOwners: @ArthurMa1978) was also evaluated but not reached since line 622 matched first.
</details>
